name: Dublin
intro: Dublin weddings sit at the higher end of the Irish market, a mix of grand city hotels, country-house venues just outside the M50, and converted warehouse-style spaces in the city itself. The bar for a wedding band in Dublin is high: couples have seen good live music and won't accept thin backing-track sets.
venues: Powerscourt House (just over the Wicklow border)
Tankardstown House (just over the Meath border)

name: Cork
intro: Cork wedding venues are a balance of coastal, country-house, and city luxury, Castlemartyr, Fota Island, Ballymaloe, Hayfield Manor. The county's wedding scene rewards musicianship; Cork couples tend to choose live bands over DJ-only receptions at a higher rate than the national average.

name: Galway
intro: Galway weddings split between west-of-Ireland castle settings (Ashford, Ballynahinch), city venues, and Connemara country houses. The atmosphere tends toward warm-and-loud rather than stately-and-quiet, which rewards bands that can build energy and hold a packed floor.
venues: Ashford Castle (just over the Mayo border)

name: Kerry
intro: Kerry's destination wedding scene is one of Ireland's most distinctive, Killarney lakeside venues, Dingle peninsula intimacy, the Park Hotel Kenmare's exclusivity. Travel matters here, both for guests and bands; couples value the kind of band that justifies the journey.

name: Limerick
intro: Limerick is anchored by Adare Manor, one of Ireland's most exacting wedding venues, and complemented by Dromoland just over the Clare border. The Limerick wedding standard skews high; bands need to bring real musicianship rather than wedding-set autopilot.

name: Mayo
intro: Mayo is Ashford Castle country, the flagship Irish destination wedding venue, set on the shores of Lough Corrib. Beyond Ashford, Mayo offers country-house and seaside-hotel options. The county's wedding scene draws couples from across Ireland and abroad.

name: Donegal
intro: Donegal weddings happen against some of Ireland's most dramatic coast and country backdrops. Lough Eske Castle leads the luxury end; the county also offers boutique and beach-adjacent options. Travel logistics matter, but a well-chosen band turns the journey north-west into part of the story.

name: Wicklow
intro: Wicklow is Dublin's preferred wedding county, Powerscourt House dominates, complemented by Druids Glen, Tinakilly Country House, and Brooklodge. Estate-and-marquee venues dominate, which means bands need both refinement and party-band energy.

name: Clare
intro: Clare is anchored by Dromoland Castle and a coast of West Clare wedding hotels and houses, Vaughan Lodge, Old Ground, the Falls. The wedding crowd skews mixed-generation and the bands chosen reflect that: versatile, six-decade repertoires that suit both grandparents and the late-night dance floor.

name: Tipperary
intro: Tipperary's wedding venues include Dundrum House, Ballyseede Castle (just over the Kerry border), and Cashel Palace, a careful balance of castle, country house, and historic-hotel style. The county's wedding scene leans toward warm, characterful celebrations rather than corporate polish.

name: Waterford
intro: Waterford weddings centre on Faithlegg House, Cliff House, and Mount Congreve. A coastal-leaning county with both period-house formality and contemporary boutique options. Bands chosen here tend toward versatile and energetic rather than stately.

name: Wexford
intro: Wexford is one of Ireland's quietly excellent wedding counties, Marlfield House, Brooklodge (just over the Wicklow border), Ferrycarrig. The wedding scene leans warm, coastal, and friendly, with a wedding crowd that responds well to harmony-driven bands.

name: Kilkenny
intro: Kilkenny's wedding circuit is led by Mount Juliet Estate, with Lyrath Estate and Newpark Hotel rounding out the higher end. Country-estate style dominates and bands chosen here tend toward the polished, big-vocal end of the spectrum.

name: Carlow
intro: Carlow's wedding scene is anchored by the Lord Bagenal Inn, Mount Wolseley, and Step House Hotel, boutique-leaning rather than castle-dominant. The bands chosen here lean toward feel-good and warm rather than stately-formal.

name: Kildare
intro: Kildare's wedding venue circuit is led by The K Club, Carton House and Barberstown Castle, estate luxury within an easy distance of Dublin. The wedding crowd skews ambitious and the bands need the scale to match.

name: Meath
intro: Meath is the home of Tankardstown House, Bellinter House and Slane Castle, a county that punches above its weight in boutique-luxury wedding venues. Couples here choose bands with attention to detail.

name: Louth
intro: Louth weddings centre on Ballymascanlon House, Darver Castle, and the Crowne Plaza Dundalk. A mix of country-house and modern-hotel options, with a wedding crowd that responds well to high-energy bands.

name: Cavan
intro: Cavan's wedding scene is led by Cabra Castle and Farnham Estate, castle character paired with relaxed-luxury hotels. The county is an easy reach from Dublin and Belfast, which gives weddings a strong mixed-crowd energy.

name: Monaghan
intro: Monaghan is Castle Leslie country, one of Ireland's most characterful wedding estates. A 1,000-acre country setting that rewards warm, harmony-led bands more than corporate polish.

name: Leitrim
intro: Leitrim weddings happen at Lough Rynn Castle, Bush Hotel, and a handful of country-house venues. A quieter wedding scene than the larger counties but no less demanding on band quality.

name: Sligo
intro: Sligo's wedding scene is led by Markree Castle, Glasshouse Hotel, and Castle Dargan, a beautiful corner of the west coast with bands chosen for character rather than stadium energy.

name: Roscommon
intro: Roscommon weddings centre on Kilronan Castle, Glasson Lakehouse and a smaller circuit of country-house venues. The wedding crowd skews warm and friendly, feel-good bands tend to land best.

name: Longford
intro: Longford's smaller wedding circuit is led by Viewmount House and the Longford Arms, boutique and characterful rather than stately. Bands chosen here lean toward feel-good and harmony-driven.

name: Westmeath
intro: Westmeath weddings are anchored by Mullingar Park Hotel, Bloomfield House, and a handful of country-house venues around Athlone. A solid mid-luxury wedding scene with versatile bands the natural fit.

name: Offaly
intro: Offaly weddings centre on Kinnitty Castle and the Bridge House Hotel, character venues with a mid-luxury wedding crowd. The county's wedding scene rewards warm, harmony-led bands.

name: Laois
intro: Laois is the home of Ballyfin Demesne, Ireland's most exclusive wedding venue, plus a smaller circuit of country-house wedding options. Ballyfin's standard sets the tone for the county.

name: Antrim
intro: Antrim weddings span Belfast city luxury, the Antrim Coast Road's dramatic backdrops, and country-house options just outside the city. Couples here regularly book Republic-of-Ireland bands for their range and quality.

name: Down
intro: Down has a strong wedding scene around Newcastle, the Slieve Donard, and the rolling Down countryside. Couples here look for live bands that handle the mixed-generation crowd well.

name: Armagh
intro: Armagh's wedding scene is anchored by Killeavy Castle and a handful of country-house venues. Smaller wedding market but rewards bands with character.

name: Tyrone
intro: Tyrone weddings span country-house venues, hotels, and traditional Irish settings. The Cohannon Inn, Lough Erne, and Blessingbourne are popular options.

name: Fermanagh
intro: Fermanagh's wedding scene is led by Lough Erne Resort, a Ryder Cup-style luxury venue with a strong international wedding crowd. Plus a quieter circuit of country houses and lakeside hotels.

name: Derry
intro: Derry/Londonderry weddings range from urban venues in the city to country-house options across the county. Live wedding bands from across the island travel to Derry regularly.
